## Get the current operating system {.unnumbered}


### Description

Returns the name of the current operating system in lowercase.
This is a simple wrapper around <code>Sys.info()[["sysname"]]</code>.



### Usage

<pre><code class='language-R'>system_os()
</code></pre>


### Details

This function is used with testthat's <code>variant</code> parameter in
<code>testthat::expect_snapshot_file()</code> to create OS-specific snapshots
when file formats produce different output across platforms.

Common use cases:


<ul>
<li> RDS files: Binary serialization format that can differ across OS

</li>
<li> Platform-specific numeric computations (e.g., MCMC algorithms)

</li>
<li> Files with platform-specific line endings or encodings

</li></ul>



### Value

A character string: &quot;linux&quot;, &quot;darwin&quot; (macOS), or &quot;windows&quot;



### Examples
```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
library("snapr")

# Use with expect_snapshot_file for OS-specific snapshots
expect_snapshot_object(my_object, name = "test", variant = system_os())



```
